manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: lash, fly, jar, fry, slack, stalk, jam, buff, stick.
Your opponent's team words are: grave, bluff, strand, blow, bull, bolt, settle, vault.
Distractor words are: trace, spill, scale, pit, lime, beetle, squash.
The kill words are: pool.

message.content:
, stickCLUE: kitchen
TARGETS: fry, jam, jar, stick
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue and list of target words we intend partner to guess. Must not be any of listed words or parts of them. We want to cover many of our team's words: lash, fly, jar, fry, slack, stalk, jam, buff, stick.

We need a clue that semantically relates to multiple of these. Perhaps "cooking" relates to fry, jam, jar (preserves), maybe "sticky"? But sticky is similar to jam, stick. "Sticky" can't be a word? It's okay but it's related. Can't contain parts of words. "Sticky" contains "stick"? That's part of a target word, maybe disallowed because clue cannot contain parts of them. It says cannot contain parts of them. So "sticky" contains "stick". That likely disallowed.

Perhaps "adhesive" relates to stick, lash? Not strongly. "Insect" relates to fly, stalk? maybe not.

"Breakfast" relates to fry, jam, jar? Maybe. "Breakfast" not contain parts. Could be good: fry (eggs), jam (toast), jar (jam jar). Also "lash"? no. "Slack"? no. "Stalk"? no. "Buff"? maybe buff breakfast? Not.

Maybe "puzzle"? No.

We could give multiple clues across turns, but we need single clue now. Should aim for a few words.

What about "cooking"? Relates to fry, jam, jar (preserve). Also "buff" as in "buff" cooking? No. "stick" could be "cooking stick" like kebab? maybe not.

Could give clue "insect" for fly, stalk (like insect stalk?) hmm.

Maybe "tool" relates to stick, lash (as tool), jar (container), buff (polish tool). Could be.

But must avoid opponent words like bluff (contains "buff" as part), so clue "tool" might lead to opponent "bluff"? "Bluff" includes "buff"? Actually "buff" is our word, but "bluff" is opponent; clue "tool" could be ambiguous.

We need avoid kill word pool.

Maybe choose clue "preserve": relates to jar (preserve), jam (preserve), maybe "stalk" as in "preserve a plant"? Not.

Better: clue "stickiness": relates to jam, stick, maybe lash? Not.

Let's see which combinations are safe: "fry", "jam", "jar" are food preservation. "buff" is to polish. "slack", "stalk", "lash", "fly", "stick". Could give clue "garden": relates to stalk, fly (garden flies), jar (garden jars?), maybe not.

Maybe "cable": relates to lash (to lash together cables), slack (slack cable), stick? No.

Clue "rope": relates to lash, slack (slack rope), stick (rope stick??). Also "fly" as "fly rope"? maybe not.

Clue "kitchen": relates to fry, jam, jar, maybe stick (cooking stick), buff (buff a pan). Could be.

Let's choose "kitchen". It doesn't contain any target words. It relates to fry, jam, jar, stick maybe. That's 4.

Provide target list: fry, jam, jar, stick.

We need to format exactly.

CLUE: kitchen
TARGETS: fry, jam, jar
